title: citrullus lanatus subsp. vulgaris
intro: Citrullus lanatus subsp. vulgaris, commonly known as the watermelon, is a subspecies of the C. lanatus plant and belongs to the Cucurbitaceae family. This plant is known for its edible fruit, rich in water, vitamins, and minerals, which possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming watermelon include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing essential nutrients for a healthy diet. Citrulline, a compound found in watermelon, can be extracted and used in herbal preparations, such as supplements and teas, to promote relaxation and reduce muscle cramps.
